title: Adazian Liebke

{{Quote|I'm really getting tired of these assassins and bounty hunters mistaking me for a holotarget or something…|[[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] Adazian Liebke, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]|<ref name="air" />}}
[[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] '''Adazian Liebke''' was a [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] and military [[Officer/Legends|officer]]. After graduating at the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]], Liebke and many of his classmates joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Liebke served in the Alliance ground forces and obtained fame as a member of the [[Eclipse Team]]. He also worked on the [[Sandwind group|Sandwind Team]] and wrote intelligence reports on threats to the Alliance for the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]]—many of those were on Liebke's personal enemies who had survived previous hostile encounters with him, including [[Callandri]], [[Ssach'thirix]], [[Ther-das]] and the [[Jundland Banshee]].

After the end of the Rebel Alliance, Lieutenant Commander Liebke joined the [[New Republic Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and survived an assassination attempt staged by his former partner turned fugitive [[Ma'w'shiye]]. Commander Liebke then became a member of a special team to track Ma'w'shiye.

==Biography==
Adazian Liebke was a [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="aj6" /> born on the Weequay [[Planet/Legends|homeworld]] [[Sriluur/Legends|Sriluur]]<ref name="air" /> to a family with a long history of military service. Following the family tradition, Liebke became a soldier at his coming of age.<ref name="aj6" />

Liebke studied at a fringe survival school,<ref name="air" /><ref name="aj6" /> the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]], where the headmaster [[Barosa Warren]] and his team taught students to thrive in hostile ecosystems by providing one of those environments on the planet [[Thrantin]]<ref name="air" /> during a course of almost five years.<ref name="aj6" /> Liebke's dozen-individual team, the [[Twilight Class]], including assorted specialists of different species who learned to work as a team while at the same time breaking a number of previous records.<ref name="air" /> During this time, Liebke and his classmate, the deranged [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodran]] [[Xenon Nnaksta|Xenon "Xe" Nnaksta]], met and became best friends.<ref name="air" /><ref name="aj6" />

In [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]],<ref name="air" /><ref name="aj6" /> Liebke and the rest of their class received their graduation assignment: An expedition through the several terrain types of Thrantin during several days, without any kind of advanced technology. Led by Liebke's classmate [[Sisquoc]], the team passed their test in 104 hours, breaking the previous record by more than a day, although they were exhausted at that point. From that point on, having been a member of the Twilight Class became a prestigious background.<ref name="air" />

During that time, Sriluur was shaken by the [[Houk–Weequay conflicts]], a local problem between the native Weequay and the immigrant [[Houk/Legends|Houks]]. Adazian Liebke returned to Sriluur and joined the [[Dnalvec Militia]], in the Weequay side.<ref name="air" /><ref name="aj6" /> During his service as a wilderness fighter,<ref name="air" /> Liebke became a [[Raquor'daan/Legends|dark wolf]] handler,<ref name="aj6" /> and owner of two dozen dark wolves. He also made two good friends at the Militia, with whom he kept contact.<ref name="air" /> Liebke saw several of his partners die to protect Weequay territories such as the city of [[Dnalvec]], [[Copper Coast]] and the industrial [[Meirm City]] from the Houks.<ref name="aj6" />

Eventually, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] stepped on Sriluur to finish the Conflicts. They established a number of garrisons in the areas that the Weequays had tried to protect, much to Liebke's chagrin. Liebke then decided to pack his belongings, secure his best wolfpack, make an offer at the [[Obsidian Shrine]], and leave Sriluur.<ref name="aj6" />

Some people suspected that Liebke had spent a brief time working either as a security guard or as an assistant at an experimental [[Cyborg/Legends|cybernetics]] laboratory, but the statement could not be confirmed and, in later years, Liebke refused to talk about this.<ref name="aj6" />

Soon after the end of the Houk-Weequay Conflicts, Liebke and his two friends from the Dnalvec Militia, as well as several of his classmates from the GOSS,<ref name="air" /> joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], also called the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="air" /><ref name="aj6" /> Liebke became a member of its branch of [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]].<ref name="gg12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref>

During an unexpected trip to the [[Pii system/Legends|Pii system]], Liebke met his former classmate and friend Nnaksta working as a manager of the [[Greel Wood Logging Corporation]] on [[Pii III]]. Nnaksta agreed to shelter Liebke so that the Empire would not find him. During that time, Liebke convinced Nnaksta to join the Alliance. From that moment on, Liebke and Nnaksta worked together in several missions, frequently arguing on who of them would drive the [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]]: Nnaksta's reckless approach to the task harmed the vehicles and sometimes the passengers, but was clearly faster than Liebke's traditional driving.<ref name="aj6" />

After a time as a refugee at [[Pii IV]], Liebke became a wilderness fighter for the Alliance Special Forces at [[New Kisge]] and a [[Major/Legends|Major]] in the Alliance ground forces. He was also the contact to send training teams to Pii IV, where he specifically asked to use rough terrain to train infantry units. Liebke was also in charge of integrating Rebel refugees by hiding them in the working force on Pii III.<ref name="aj6" />

Later, Liebke became a [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] and served along [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Ma'w'shiye]], one of his former classmates at the GOSS.<ref name="aj4" /> Soon, Liebke became one of the most distinguished Weequays in the Rebel Alliance, and he became notorious as a member of the infamous [[Twilight Squadron (Rebel)|Twilight Squadron]] in the equally-disreputable [[Eclipse Team]]. He was also famous because of his use of his [[Raquor'daan/Legends|raquor'daans]] in missions.<ref name="air" />

Liebke was assigned to the [[Sandwind group|Sandwind Team]] as interim head, when agent [[N'tain Xalis]] was at [[Elus sector/Legends|Elus sector]]. On [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], he noticed that the vandalic activities of the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] nicknamed the [[Jundland Banshee]] were a real problem to the Sandwind cell and wrote a report on him.<ref name="air" />

A partner of fellow Rebel officers [[Alton Lochner]] and [[Tirranna]], Liebke was having drinks with them on the [[Brawl and Grill]] establishment, in [[Trader's Quarter]], [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]. Suddenly, assassin [[Callandri]] entered the bar shooting, on an attempt to kill Lochner. As Tirranna and Liebke were unaware of a good reason for Lochner to die, they worked together saving Lochner's life. Tirranna's actions were essential in the rescue. Liebke would be later consulted by Rebel officer [[Vanden Willard/Legends|Vanden Willard]] when Willard wanted to write a report on Callandri.<ref name="air" />

Liebke cooperated with [[Xenon Nnaksta]], trying to get supplies for the Rebel cell on Pii III. They were found by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Ssach'thirix]], who tried to capture them. Believing him to be an Imperial bounty hunter, Nnaksta and Liebke asked for reinforcements. With help in the form of [[Kichiir]] and others in the area, Ssach'thirix was beaten and humiliated. Later, Liebke would write a report on the threat of Ssach'thirix, specifying that he was bored of bounty hunters and assassins taking him for a holo-target.<ref name="air" />

Liebke was a member of the Rebel Alliance [[Task Force on Alliance Security|Task Force]], writing essential reports about some important threats to the Rebel Alliance. Besides of Callandri and Ssach'thirix, Liebke also wrote on [[Ther-das]], the [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]] swordsbeing: He specifically noticed that Ther-das's past addiction was no longer a problem to the fencer on [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], and that the incident on [[Teloc Ol-sen]] was a good proof the threat Ther-das posed.<ref name="air" />

After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Lieutenant Commander Liebke went on to serve the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] as an officer. Ma'w'shiye, on the other hand, had deserted and began a series of [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] attempts on the lives of his previous teammates, including Liebke. Liebke did survive, and then joined a special team formed to track the fugitive. Liebke became one of the officers leading this team.<ref name="aj4" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Liebke.jpg|thumb|left|Liebke was a man of many talents.]]
Adazian Liebke was a trained survival expert,<ref name="air" /> especially in desert conditions. He was in good shape and was a qualified rider and climber, having also honed his stealth and searching skills. He was also a good fighter using either his naked hands, melee weapons or, to a lesser extent, [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] or personal and vehicle-mounted [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons. His weapons of choice were a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] and a [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]], the weapon he used best. He also wore a protective [[battle vest]] and carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="aj6" />

Liebke was also a good pilot, better than his friend Nnaksta, but Nnaksta was indifferent to the state of the vehicle and of the passengers, and thus the Vodran was faster than Liebke. Liebke's specific piloting skills focused on ground vehicles such as [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]] and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]]—which he could also repair—but he had had some experience operating [[Starship/Legends|starship]] guns. Liebke had technical skills on the fields of security, demolitions and programming and repairing computers, as well as some first aid training.<ref name="aj6" />

Having served in the Dnalvec Militia, Liebke was very familiar with its bureaucracy and, to a lesser extent, with the bureaucracy of other organizations. He had studied about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species, planetary systems and law enforcement, as was comfortable moving through the criminal underworld. Liebke was a good, experienced commander, and sometimes an intimidating person.<ref name="aj6" />

Liebke was also notable as a handler of dark wolves,<ref name="gg12" /> having specific scholarly training in the area. Liebke commonly carried with him [[tail stinger wrap]]s and two [[dark wolf muzzle]]s.<ref name="aj6" />

Like other Weequays, Liebke generated pheromones that he could use to communicate with other Weequays from his clan without uttering a word. Liebke was unlike many Weequays while dealing with other Rebels: He was friendly in general, but only really open with close partners who had survived several ordeals with him,<ref name="aj6" /> while other Weequays tended to be quiet, violent, unfriendly people.<ref name="gg12" />

==Behind the scenes==
Adazian Liebke was first mentioned in the article [[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' #4]] (November [[1994]]) and written by [[Craig Robert Carey]]. Information on Liebke was expanded in the article ''[[The Greel Wood Haven]]'', also by Carey published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' #6]] (May [[1995]]). This article included the first image of Liebke, drawn by [[David Day]].
